layers.name: Policy Layer
layers.description: Deep reinforcement learning agent implementations, policy definitions, and training/testing scripts.

layers.name: Environment Layer
layers.description: ROS 2 environment interfaces and interaction logic for the DRL agent.

layers.name: Utility Layer
layers.description: Helper scripts for plotting, metrics calculation, data buffers, and architecture visualization.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Hyperparameters, environment settings, and ROS 2 package configuration files.

layers.name: Launch Layer
layers.description: ROS 2 launch files serving as entry points to start the agent and related nodes.

tour.title: Training Entry Point
tour.description: Start here to see how the ANHCAPE agent is initialized and trained within the ROS 2 ecosystem. This script bridges the deep reinforcement learning loop with ROS 2 execution, acting as the main entry point for policy optimization.
tour.languageLesson: In ROS 2, nodes use an executor to process callbacks. Deep reinforcement learning scripts often run the training loop in a separate thread to avoid blocking ROS 2 communication.

tour.title: Configuration Layer
tour.description: Before diving into the policy, it's essential to understand the configurations that govern it. These YAML files specify the machine learning hyperparameters, environment thresholds, and training setup that the entry point script loads.

tour.title: Core DRL Agent
tour.description: This is the core implementation of the ANHCAPE agent, based on TD3. Here you will find the Adaptive Exploration decay, Q-value clipping, and the Dual Encoder system as mentioned in the project overview.
tour.languageLesson: PyTorch neural networks define their architecture in the `__init__` method and the forward pass in `forward()`. Separating the architecture definition from the reinforcement learning logic keeps the codebase modular.

tour.title: Environment Interaction
tour.description: For the agent to learn, it must interact with Gazebo. These scripts implement the ROS 2 environment wrappers, translating ROS messages into state representations and RL actions into ROS velocity commands.

tour.title: State and Memory Utilities
tour.description: Off-policy algorithms rely on experience replay. The buffer script handles storing and sampling transitions, while the point cloud utilities transform raw LiDAR data into the appropriate state arrays for the Dual Encoder system.

tour.title: Testing and Evaluation
tour.description: Once trained, the agent is tested using these evaluation scripts and launch files. They load the best checkpoints and run the agent through predefined start-goal pairs to assess navigation performance in simulation.
tour.languageLesson: ROS 2 Launch files in Python offer a programmatic way to start multiple nodes, set parameters, and remap topics, providing much more flexibility than the older XML-based launch format.

tour.title: Analysis and Visualization
tour.description: The final piece of the architecture involves analyzing results. These utilities and Jupyter notebooks visualize the training metrics, explore neural network structures, and overlay robot trajectories on the simulation map.
